title: AI and Industrial Development Deep Research Report
abstract: This report examines how AI is moving from model capability breakthroughs into large-scale industrial deployment, covering the path toward AGI, multimodal and agent capabilities, infrastructure and workflow orchestration, sector-specific applications, and the practical implications for commercialization, governance, and long-term productivity transformation.
pdfUrl: ./AIGC发展/AI and Industrial Development Deep Research Report (English).pdf

title: AI Writing Deep Research Report
abstract: This report examines AI writing as it shifts from sentence-level generation toward platform-based creative production, covering regulation, fiction, scripts, responsibility, official data, industry variables, and practical implications for creators, platforms, and investors.

title: Suno Research Report
abstract: This report examines Suno's evolution from an AI songwriting tool into a music operating system, covering its product workflow, competitive position, business model, copyright and regulatory risks, original strategic concepts, and practical implications for creators, platforms, rights holders, and investors.

title: Seedance 2.0 Research Report
abstract: This report examines Seedance 2.0 as an industrial-grade multimodal audio-video generation system rather than a standalone text-to-video tool. It analyzes the model's unified reference and production workflow capabilities, the market and policy context behind faster content creation demand, and the strategic shift from competing on model effects alone to building integrated infrastructure for assets, templates, compliance, and scalable content production.

title: Token Consumption Studies Report
abstract: This report examines token consumption as an operational resource for enterprise AI, framing tokens not only as linguistic units but also as cost, throughput, budget, and governance units. It analyzes the supply-side expansion of compute capacity, the demand-side rise of large-scale AI usage, and the management systems organizations need to measure, budget, route, and govern token consumption effectively.

title: NotebookLM Research Report
abstract: This report examines NotebookLM's product positioning, capability evolution, application scenarios, and competitive outlook, highlighting its core mechanisms, practical value, and deployment implications.

title: AI Healthy Aging Deep Research Report
abstract: This report examines AI-enabled healthy aging, mapping key technologies, application scenarios, care models, industry trends, and practical implementation pathways for research, service design, and decision-making in aging-related contexts.
pdfUrl: ./AIGC发展/AI Healthy Aging Deep Research Report (English).pdf

title: OpenClaw Swarm Research Report
abstract: This report examines OpenClaw swarm collaboration, mapping multi-agent coordination patterns, organizational evolution, and practical implementation paths for research and decision-making.

title: Claude Code Passive Open-Source Incident: In-Depth Research Report
abstract: This report provides an in-depth assessment of the Claude Code passive open-source incident based on public evidence and governance frameworks as of March 31, 2026. It argues the event is best understood as a release-governance failure and source-map exposure in the runtime/tooling layer rather than model-weight leakage, then analyzes impacts across IP, security, competition, and trust. The report further proposes a practical control framework for AI coding systems, including artifact-release validation, permission boundary hardening, network isolation, plugin/MCP tiered governance, and auditable incident-response mechanisms.
pdfUrl: ./AI Agent/Claude Code Passive Open-Source Incident: In-Depth Research Report (English).pdf

title: Why Anthropic Became the Fastest-Iterating AI Team
abstract: This report argues that Anthropic’s speed comes from an "AI builds AI" bootstrap flywheel rather than isolated model wins. It maps a closed-loop system linking model advances, Claude Code-enabled engineering throughput, rapid product release, and high-frequency internal dogfooding feedback. The analysis further identifies five amplifiers—shared stack, prototype-as-spec, parallelized governance, coding focus, and organizational short-loop execution—and explains why compute alone cannot account for Anthropic’s iteration advantage. The report concludes that Anthropic’s moat is increasingly toolized (Claude Code + Agent SDK + workflow/runtime integration), making its lead a system-level speed advantage likely to persist if the flywheel keeps compounding.
pdfUrl: ./AI Agent/Why Anthropic Became the Fastest-Iterating AI Team (English).pdf

title: Harness Engineering Report
abstract: This report argues that Harness Engineering is an operating-system-layer discipline for highly autonomous and governable AI systems. It organizes the discussion through terminology, evidence, structure, and implementation, and shows a shift from prompt-centric generation to workflow-centric orchestration with tools, multi-step actions, and dynamic path selection. Using software and R&D as the strongest evidence-based starting point, it outlines practical routines across coding, testing, maintenance, and documentation, and concludes that the key scarcity is institutionalizing human judgment into a governable AI production system.

title: OpenClaw Management
abstract: This report examines OpenClaw management as an organizational operating problem in the AI-agent era, rather than a pure model-performance problem. It argues that enterprises should treat agents as bounded execution entities and build management around authority boundaries, memory governance, escalation loops, auditability, and cost control. Using a ten-module framework, the report covers industrial context, organizational mechanisms, operational accounting, risk governance, and roadmap design, and proposes a staged deployment path from single-point pilots to role-based rollout and fleet-level governance dashboards.

title: AI Agent Management
abstract: This report reframes AI adoption as an organizational management problem rather than a pure model-capability problem. It proposes an AI-agent management framework centered on controllability, accountability, and stable execution, covering boundaries of management objects, multi-agent complexity, organizational structure, strategic inventory, index systems, unit economics, and infrastructure constraints (compute, data, supervision, and energy). The report argues that long-term competitiveness depends on turning model capability into institutional operating systems, where trusted throughput and governance capacity scale together, and it outlines practical pathways for institutionalization and standardization.

title: OpenClaw Domestication or Evolution (English)
abstract: This report examines whether OpenClaw should be steered toward controlled domestication or autonomous evolution, framing the issue as a layered governance and capability question. It compares domestication, adaptation, and strategic self-optimization paths, and discusses practical mechanisms including permission governance, safety controls, execution-loop design, and feedback-driven optimization. The report provides an operational lens for designing, deploying, and governing AI-agent systems in real-world organizational settings.

title: OpenOctopus: A Manufacturing and Sensor Industry Collaboration Platform
abstract: This report introduces OpenOctopus as a collaboration platform for manufacturing and the sensor value chain. It focuses on an end-to-end workflow from requirement clarification to engineering analysis, project delivery, and continuous data accumulation. It also outlines core moats, phased commercialization, and the current market window for industrial AI deployment.
pdfUrl: ./AI Agent/OpenOctopus Manufacturing and Sensor Industry Collaboration Platform (English).pdf

title: OpenClaw as a Digital Employee Operating Layer: Architecture, Gaps, and Practical Pathways
abstract: This report repositions OpenClaw from a personal assistant gateway to a lightweight operating layer for digital employees. It analyzes core architecture and execution capabilities (multi-channel ingress, tool invocation, event-driven workflows, cron scheduling, and skill distribution), evaluates key gaps against enterprise-grade control-plane requirements (identity, governance, observability, and orchestration), and proposes a self-hosted, execution-first pathway grounded in practical scenarios such as social monitoring, patent workflows, and in-depth research delivery.

title: Token Economics Panorama Report (English Edition)
abstract: This report presents a panoramic analysis of token economics in the AI era, spanning global compute infrastructure, inference economics, China’s compute industry, labor-market and organizational transformation, capital-market logic, and geopolitical implications. It highlights key trends such as sharply falling inference costs alongside rising total spending, increasing K-shaped polarization, and the growing coupling between compute power and energy systems.

title: OpenClaw Shrimp Farming Panorama Report (English Edition)
abstract: This report provides a panoramic analysis of OpenClaw-enabled shrimp farming, covering industry context, technology stack, operational workflows, key challenges, and practical implementation pathways for scalable and data-driven aquaculture.

title: AI Short Drama Development Report (English)
abstract: This report examines the current landscape of AI short drama, maps the end-to-end industry chain, and analyzes production, distribution, and monetization pathways, highlighting key challenges and practical opportunities for deployment.

title: Global General-Purpose Agent Competition Report 2026
abstract: 本报告围绕《Global General-Purpose Agent Competition Report 2026》展开，聚焦AI Agent相关议题，系统梳理核心进展、关键问题与落地路径，供研究与决策参考。

title: OpenClaw 3.11 Update Report (English)
abstract: English update briefing on OpenClaw 3.11, summarizing key improvements, workflow updates, and practical adoption guidance.

title: OpenClaw and the Intelligent Economy
abstract: 本报告围绕《OpenClaw and the Intelligent Economy》展开，聚焦OpenClaw相关议题，系统梳理核心进展、关键问题与落地路径，供研究与决策参考。

title: OpenClaw Strategic Analysis
abstract: 本报告围绕 OpenClaw 的战略定位、市场机会、产品路径与落地策略进行系统分析，面向研究与应用场景提供可执行的判断框架。

title: AIGC发展研究3.0发布版
abstract: 本报告由清华大学清新研究团队发布，聚焦生成式人工智能（AIGC）的产业发展、技术趋势、社会影响与治理机制。深入辨析AIGC在多元场景中的落地应用与价值创造。

title: AIGC发展研究1.0版
abstract: 作为本系列的第一份报告，初步梳理了元宇宙与AIGC的基本概念、内容生态及初步发展的机遇与挑战。
